column1: New Zealand and United States are in discussions to sign a bilateral security agreement specific to sharing military intelligence and data. The goal of the agreement would be to make the militaries of both states more interoperable. However, to achieve the most benefit, this would require linking together databases that hold important classified material. There has been some push back in the military and intelligence bureaucracies of New Zealand about fully linking together this important digital information. However, proponents suggest that such information sharing will be critical for achieving joint security interests. How should New Zealand approach this proposed agreement?
column2: Sign the security agreement with United States
column3: Do not sign the security agreement with United States
